changes_made: Changed k8s_rbac_role_binding_least_privilege to k8s_rbac_least_privilege_enforcement for consistency with existing patterns
review_notes: The control 'Identifier Management | Identify User Status' is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves managing user identifiers and access control, which aligns with RBAC configurations in Kubernetes. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but one function name was adjusted for consistency. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to RBAC.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of terms like 'least_privilege_enforcement' across similar checks
title: Identifier Management | Identify User Status
step1_reasoning: The control 'Identifier Management | Identify User Status' is focused on managing user identifiers and ensuring that access is appropriately controlled. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that RBAC is properly configured to prevent unauthorized or overly privileged access. Specifically, ensuring that anonymous access is disabled, service accounts do not automatically mount tokens unless necessary, and role bindings enforce least privilege are relevant checks.
